questions.text: If x and y are in direct proportion, which of the following is always true?
questions.explanation: In direct proportion, x/y = k (constant). As x increases, y increases proportionally.

questions.text: If x and y are in inverse proportion, which of the following is always true?
questions.explanation: In inverse proportion, x × y = k (constant). As x increases, y decreases such that their product stays the same.

questions.text: If 6 pens cost ₹90, what do 10 pens cost?
questions.explanation: Cost per pen = 90/6 = ₹15. Cost of 10 pens = 15 × 10 = ₹150. (Direct proportion)

questions.text: If 8 workers can complete a job in 15 days, how many days will 12 workers take?
questions.explanation: Workers × Days = constant (inverse proportion). 8 × 15 = 12 × d. d = 120/12 = 10 days.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of direct proportion?

options.a: Speed and time for a fixed distance
options.b: Number of workers and time to complete a job
options.c: Distance travelled and time taken at constant speed
options.d: Number of pipes and time to fill a tank

questions.explanation: At constant speed, distance = speed × time. As time increases, distance increases proportionally — direct proportion.

questions.text: A car covers 180 km in 3 hours. How far will it travel in 5 hours at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = 180/3 = 60 km/h. Distance in 5 hours = 60 × 5 = 300 km. (Direct proportion)

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of inverse proportion?

options.a: Cost of apples and number of apples
options.b: Distance and time at constant speed
options.c: Number of books and total cost
options.d: Number of taps and time to fill a tank

questions.explanation: More taps → less time to fill the tank. As one increases, the other decreases — inverse proportion.

questions.text: If x₁/y₁ = x₂/y₂, then x and y are in:
questions.explanation: If x₁/y₁ = x₂/y₂ = k (constant), then x and y are in direct proportion.

questions.text: A factory produces 450 items in 9 hours. How many items will it produce in 6 hours at the same rate?
questions.explanation: Rate = 450/9 = 50 items/hour. In 6 hours = 50 × 6 = 300 items. (Direct proportion)

questions.text: If x₁y₁ = x₂y₂, then x and y are in:
questions.explanation: If x₁y₁ = x₂y₂ = k (constant product), then x and y are in inverse proportion.

questions.text: 20 men can dig a trench in 12 days. How many men are needed to dig it in 8 days?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 20 × 12 = n × 8. n = 240/8 = 30 men.

questions.text: If the cost of 5 m of cloth is ₹375, the cost of 8 m of the same cloth is:
questions.explanation: Cost per metre = 375/5 = ₹75. Cost of 8 m = 75 × 8 = ₹600.

questions.text: A tap fills a tank in 6 hours. How long will 3 such taps take to fill the same tank?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 1 tap × 6 hours = 3 taps × t. t = 6/3 = 2 hours.

questions.text: If 4 kg of rice costs ₹160, how much does 7 kg cost?
questions.explanation: Cost per kg = 160/4 = ₹40. Cost of 7 kg = 40 × 7 = ₹280.

questions.text: A train travels 288 km in 4 hours. In how many hours will it travel 504 km at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = 288/4 = 72 km/h. Time = 504/72 = 7 hours.

questions.text: If 15 days of work for 1 person equals 5 days for x persons, then x = ?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 1 × 15 = x × 5. x = 15/5 = 3 persons.

questions.text: A photograph measuring 6 cm × 4 cm is enlarged so that the longer side becomes 15 cm. The shorter side of the enlarged photo is:
questions.explanation: Direct proportion: 6/15 = 4/x. x = (4 × 15)/6 = 60/6 = 10 cm.

questions.text: If 36 men can do a piece of work in 25 days, in how many days will 15 men do it?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 36 × 25 = 15 × d. d = 900/15 = 60 days.

questions.text: The variable y varies directly with x. When x = 4, y = 20. When x = 7, y = ?
questions.explanation: y/x = constant = 20/4 = 5. When x = 7, y = 5 × 7 = 35.

questions.text: At ₹12.50 per metre, what length of cloth can be purchased for ₹200?
questions.explanation: Length = Total cost / Rate = 200 / 12.50 = 16 m.

questions.text: A recipe needs 2½ cups of flour to make 20 biscuits. How many cups are needed for 50 biscuits?
A garrison of 500 soldiers has food for 24 days. If 125 more soldiers join, for how many days will the food last?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 500 × 24 = 625 × d. d = 12000/625 = 19.2 days.

questions.text: A car travels at 60 km/h and takes 5 hours to reach a destination. How long will it take at 75 km/h?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion (speed × time = distance constant): 60 × 5 = 75 × t. t = 300/75 = 4 hours.

questions.text: A map scale is 1 cm : 25 km. Two cities appear 8.4 cm apart on the map. Their actual distance is:
questions.explanation: Direct proportion: 1 cm = 25 km. So 8.4 cm = 8.4 × 25 = 210 km.

questions.text: 6 pumps of the same capacity can empty a tank in 28 minutes. How many minutes will 8 such pumps take?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 6 × 28 = 8 × t. t = 168/8 = 21 minutes.

questions.text: 4 pipes of equal capacity can fill a tank in 15 hours. If 2 pipes are closed, how many hours will the remaining pipes take to fill the same tank?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 4 × 15 = 2 × t. t = 60/2 = 30 hours.

questions.text: A farmer has enough grain to feed 40 cows for 60 days. If he sells 10 cows, for how many days will the grain last?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 40 × 60 = 30 × d. d = 2400/30 = 80 days.

questions.text: A shadow of a pole 4 m high is 6 m long. At the same time, the shadow of a tree is 15 m long. What is the height of the tree?
questions.explanation: Direct proportion (same sunlight angle): 4/6 = h/15. h = (4 × 15)/6 = 60/6 = 10 m.

questions.text: A cycle wheel makes 840 revolutions in travelling 1.056 km. How many revolutions will it make in 3.3 km?
If 56 workers can build a wall in 18 days, how many days will 42 workers take to build the same wall?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 56 × 18 = 42 × d. d = 1008/42 = 24 days.

questions.text: A hostel has food for 50 students for 30 days. After 10 days, 10 more students join. For how many more days will the food last?
questions.explanation: Food consumed in 10 days by 50 students = 50 × 10 = 500 units. Remaining food = 50 × 30 - 500 = 1500 - 500 = 1000 units. Now 60 students. Days remaining = 1000/60 = 16⅔ days.

questions.text: A and B together can finish a job in 12 days. A alone takes 20 days. In how many days can B alone finish the job?
questions.explanation: A+B work per day = 1/12. A's work per day = 1/20. B's work per day = 1/12 - 1/20 = 5/60 - 3/60 = 2/60 = 1/30. B alone takes 30 days.

questions.text: A train 150 m long passes a pole in 12 seconds. How long will it take to cross a bridge 600 m long?
questions.explanation: Speed = 150/12 = 12.5 m/s. Distance to cross bridge = 150 + 600 = 750 m. Time = 750/12.5 = 60 seconds.

questions.text: A and B can do a job in 8 days together. B and C can do it in 12 days together. A and C can do it in 16 days together. How many days will A, B and C together take?
A tap fills a tank in 12 hours. Another tap drains it in 16 hours. If both are open together, how long to fill the empty tank?
questions.explanation: Filling rate = 1/12 per hour. Draining rate = 1/16 per hour. Net rate = 1/12 - 1/16 = 4/48 - 3/48 = 1/48 per hour. Time = 48 hours.

questions.text: In a school, 2/5 of the students are in the junior section. The ratio of boys to girls in junior is 3:2 and in senior is 4:3. What fraction of all students are junior girls?
questions.explanation: Junior section = 2/5 of total. In junior, girls = 2/(3+2) = 2/5 of junior. Junior girls = 2/5 × 2/5 = 4/25 of total students.

questions.text: A car uses 6 litres of petrol to travel 111 km. How many litres will it need to travel 259 km? (Answer to 1 decimal place)
questions.explanation: Direct proportion: 6/111 = x/259. x = 6 × 259/111 = 1554/111 = 14 litres.

questions.text: 24 men can dig a trench 180 m long in 18 days. How many men are needed to dig a 270 m long trench in 12 days?
questions.explanation: Work = men × days. For same depth: men and length are direct, men and days are inverse. M₂ = M₁ × (L₂/L₁) × (D₁/D₂) = 24 × (270/180) × (18/12) = 24 × 1.5 × 1.5 = 54 men.

questions.text: If 15 workers finish a task in 8 hours working at a constant rate, how many workers are needed to finish the same task in 6 hours?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 15 × 8 = n × 6. n = 120/6 = 20 workers.

questions.text: A machine fills 420 bottles in 3 hours. How many bottles will it fill in 5 hours?
questions.explanation: Direct proportion: 420/3 = x/5. x = 420 × 5/3 = 2100/3 = 700 bottles.

questions.text: A fort had food for 300 soldiers for 90 days. After 30 days, 100 soldiers were transferred out. For how many more days will the food last?
questions.explanation: Food left after 30 days = 300 × (90-30) = 300 × 60 = 18000 units. Remaining soldiers = 300 - 100 = 200. Days = 18000 / 200 = 90 days.

questions.text: A model of a ship is made to a scale of 1:500. If the length of the model is 30 cm, what is the actual length of the ship in metres?
questions.explanation: Scale 1:500 means 1 cm on model = 500 cm actual. Actual length = 30 × 500 = 15000 cm = 150 m.

questions.text: If 7 men can reap a field in 12 days, in how many days will 3 men reap the same field?
questions.explanation: Inverse proportion: 7 × 12 = 3 × d. d = 84/3 = 28 days.

questions.text: A worker earns ₹1680 in 14 days. How many days will it take him to earn ₹3600?
questions.explanation: Direct proportion: earnings/days = constant. 1680/14 = 3600/d. d = 3600 × 14/1680 = 50400/1680 = 30 days.

questions.text: A pipe can fill a tank in 20 hours. Another pipe can fill it in 30 hours. If both pipes are opened together, how many hours will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/20 + 1/30 = 3/60 + 2/60 = 5/60 = 1/12 per hour. Time = 12 hours.

questions.text: The cost of 35 chairs is ₹87500. Find the number of such chairs that can be purchased for ₹1,00,000.
questions.explanation: Cost per chair = 87500/35 = ₹2500. Number of chairs = 100000/2500 = 40 chairs.

cases.case_text: Riya's family is planning a road trip. Their car gives a mileage of 15 km per litre of petrol. The total distance of the trip is 450 km. Petrol costs ₹105 per litre. They plan to travel at a constant speed of 60 km/h.

subparts.text: How many litres of petrol will the trip require?
subparts.explanation: Litres needed = 450/15 = 30 litres. (Direct proportion: more distance, more petrol)

subparts.text: What will be the total cost of petrol for the trip?
How many hours will the trip take at 60 km/h? (Direct proportion: more speed, less time — this is actually inverse. Find the time.)

cases.case_text: A construction company has taken a contract to build a wall. Originally 90 workers were to complete the wall in 30 days. After 10 days, only 60% of the work was completed (due to some workers being sick). The company must now finish the remaining 40% of work in the remaining 20 days.

subparts.text: How much work (in %) was originally planned to be completed in the first 10 days?
subparts.explanation: At uniform rate, 10 days out of 30 days = 10/30 = 1/3 = 33.3% of work planned.

subparts.text: If 90 workers complete 60% work in 10 days, how many workers are needed to complete the remaining 40% work in 20 days?
subparts.explanation: 90 workers do 60% in 10 days → 1 worker does 60/(90×10) = 1/15 % per day. To do 40% in 20 days: n × 20 × (1/15) = 40. n = 40×15/20 = 30 workers.

subparts.text: How many extra workers must be hired compared to the original 90? (Assume some of the original workers are still working — find only the additional number needed.)
subparts.explanation: Only 30 workers are needed for the remaining work. Since 90 workers are already available, no extra workers are needed. In fact fewer workers are needed (30 < 90).
